questionHtml:
Which elements are capable of forming expanded octets?

I.   Nitrogen
II.  Phosphorus
III. Arsenic


A.  I and II only

B.  I and III only

C.  II and III only

D.  I, II and III

meta.title: Which elements are capable of forming expanded octets? I. NitrogenII. PhosphorusIII. Arsenic A. I and II only B. I and III only C. II and III only D. I, II and III
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure

Structure 2.2.13—Some atoms can form molecules in which they have an expanded octet of electrons. Visually represent Lewis formulas for species with five and six electron domains around the central atom. Deduce the electron domain geometry and the molecular geometry for these species using the VSEPR model.
